# busybox applets present on the OpenWrt target, one per line.
#
# This is the bulk of the command surface a stock OpenWrt device provides. It is
# consumed by check-device-commands.py together with openwrt-base.txt to decide
# whether a command invoked directly by the travelmate shell code actually
# exists on the device. A command on neither list (e.g. `od`, absent from the
# device busybox) is the bug class this gate catches: it works on the build host
# but fails silently at runtime on the router.
#
# Captured from the reference target GL-MT3000 (OpenWrt 25.12.2) -- the busybox
# applets are exactly the entries in /bin /sbin /usr/bin /usr/sbin that are
# symlinks to busybox:
#
#   for d in /bin /sbin /usr/bin /usr/sbin; do
#     for f in "$d"/*; do
#       case "$(readlink "$f")" in */busybox|busybox) echo "${f##*/}" ;; esac
#     done
#   done | sort -u
#
# Refresh by re-running the above on a current target and replacing this list.

[
[[
ash
awk
basename
brctl
cat
chgrp
chmod
chown
chroot
clear
cmp
cp
crond
crontab
cut
date
dd
df
dirname
dmesg
du
echo
egrep
env
expr
false
fgrep
find
flock
free
fsync
grep
gunzip
gzip
halt
head
hexdump
hwclock
id
ifconfig
kill
killall
less
ln
lock
logger
login
ls
md5sum
mkdir
mkfifo
mknod
mkswap
mktemp
mount
mv
nc
netmsg
netstat
nice
nslookup
ntpd
passwd
pgrep
pidof
ping
ping6
pivot_root
poweroff
printf
ps
pwd
readlink
reboot
reset
resize
rm
rmdir
route
sed
seq
setsid
sh
sha256sum
sleep
sort
start-stop-daemon
strings
swapoff
swapon
switch_root
sync
sysctl
tail
tar
taskset
tee
test
time
top
touch
tr
traceroute
traceroute6
true
udhcpc
umount
uname
uniq
uptime
vi
wc
which
xargs
yes
zcat
